description:
Welcome to a quick hands on tour of Kali Linux! In this 35 minute short course, you'll learn key command line skills to help you navigate and work confidently in any Linux environment—even if you’re a beginner.
What You’ll Learn:
• Where you are and who you are: Use pwd to check your current directory and whoami to find your login name.
• Moving around: Understanding the Linux file system and using cd to change directories.
• Seeing files & folders: List items in a directory with ls.
• Getting help: Explore support tools—running help, --help, and manual pages using man.
• Searching files:
o locate to quickly find file paths
o whereis to find programs
o which to see which binary will run
o find for powerful searches
o Filtering results with grep
• Working with files & directories:
o View file content using cat
o Create files (e.g. touch or the shell)
o Make directories (mkdir)
o Copy (cp), rename (mv), and delete (rm and rmdir)
• Text tools & manipulation:
o Show file beginning lines with head
o Add line numbers (nl or grep -n)
o Filter text using grep
o Learn search-and-replace using sed
At the end, check your knowledge with hands on exercises, so you can practice what you learned.
Whether you're starting with Kali Linux or another Linux distro, these core tools will set you up for success. Don’t forget to like, subscribe, and leave a comment if you found it useful!
